evidence.scientific_clarity: Ridgeline plot with 6 KDE ridges, unit-normalised, stacked with overlap=1.8, left-side coloured labels — fully satisfies the brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans and DejaVuSans-Bold; SVG text is glyph paths, not selectable; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Tableau-10 colours used; red (#e15759) and green (#59a14f) both appear in the same categorical palette, creating red-green CVD confusion risk; no explicit CVD-safe palette chosen.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(7,5) with 6 ridges at overlap=1.8 fills space reasonably; clean spines, no yticks, tight_layout applied; left margin for labels may be tight but functional.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and lists key parameters; title and x-axis label present; no legend needed; however caption states no specific scientific finding or comparison between clusters.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains Type 3 (bitmap) fonts confirmed by pdffonts; SVG encodes text as DejaVuSans path IDs rather than selectable glyphs; fonttype=42 never set in script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (51 KB), figure.png (53 KB), figure.svg (188 KB), caption.tex (369 B), gen_figure.py (2.1 KB) all present and non-trivial; all required outputs satisfied.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains plot type, KDE kernel, bandwidth (0.25), normalisation, and n≈400 per cluster; lacks a specific numerical finding or scientific claim distinguishing clusters.

overall_strengths: Figure faithfully implements the brief: 6-cluster ridgeline with stacked KDEs, unit-normalised, coloured left-side labels, clean minimal axes.
All required output files (PDF, PNG, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial in size.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 set, resulting in Type 3 bitmap fonts in the PDF and path-encoded text in the SVG — text is not selectable or editable in any vector output.
Palette includes both red and green as categorical colours, posing a red-green colorblind accessibility problem; default DejaVu Sans font used with no publication-quality font choice.
